persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: the dossier flags this as single-initial and 8 Bertram officials exist. Why it fails: the forename 'Anton' is spelled out in full in both the biography and the staff list (not a bare initial), the honour 'K.C.' is shared (bio K.C. 1913; stint records 'K.C., Kt.'), and the stint reproduces the bio's exact arc - Attorney-General of Ceylon from 1911/1912 then Chief Justice from 1918/1919, listed as 'Sir Anton Bertram' after the 1916 knighthood. Five edition-years co-occur. Full distinctive forename plus shared honour plus two unique offices defeat the namesake hypothesis.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence that a different Anton Bertram held the Ceylon attorney-generalship and chief justiceship in these years.

notes: Single-initial flag is misleading: full forename 'Anton' agrees, K.C. honour is shared, and the AG->Chief Justice arc with the knighthood matches exactly.
